new_text: ChromaDB Backup + DB Size Watchdog (Session 97). Modified 5 files: memory_server.py (_backup_database using sqlite3.backup()), chromadb_socket.py (backup() wrapper), gather.py (gather_backup()), session_end.py (backup_database with mtime-skip), boot.py (DB size watchdog <1MB). Verified: 1043 tests pass, 22.23 MB backup works, mtime-skip confirmed. Design: backup refuses if DB <1KB, atomic writes (.tmp+os.replace), threshold <1MB, all operations fail-open.

Telegram client library evaluation. Competitors: dark-send (33★, wraps Telethon, send-only), MadelineProto (3.3K★, PHP+AGPL), Unigram (4.9K★, C# GUI, not programmable), vysheng/tg (6.9K★, abandoned 2016, protocol outdated). Winner: Telethon (11.8K★, MIT, native Python, full MTProto, send/search/history, active community). Decision: Telethon correct choice for Telegram Memory Plugin.

TRv1 unit tests added to 4 files: (1) developer_fees.rs—7 tests (min_cu, proportional shares, config account, inactive program, cooldown, mixed), uses borsh::BorshSerialize, AccountSharedData::new(lamports, data.len(), owner). (2) constants.rs—6 tier tests (reward_rate, vote_weight, penalty, is_valid). (3) state.rs—Borsh round-trip, discriminator, empty data, buffer size, lock variant. (4) fee_distribution.rs—epoch 912 interpolation test.

Created shared/rate_limiter.py token bucket limiter. Presets: TOOL_RATE (10/min, burst 10), GATE_RATE (30/min, burst 30), API_RATE (60/min, burst 60). Key prefix matching (tool:/gate:/api:), persistence /dev/shm/claude-hooks/rate_limiter.json (atomic). In-memory state in _buckets, loaded at import. Public API: allow(), consume(), get_remaining(), reset(), get_all_limits()—all fail-open. Smoke test 14 assertions pass, no regressions.

Created event_bus.py pub/sub for Torus. EventType: GATE_FIRED, GATE_BLOCKED, MEMORY_QUERIED, TEST_RUN, ERROR_DETECTED, FIX_APPLIED, TOOL_CALLED. Ring buffer deque(maxlen=1000), thread-safe. API: subscribe(), unsubscribe(), publish(), get_recent(limit=50), clear(), get_stats(), configure(), load_persisted(). Ramdisk /dev/shm/claude-hooks/events.json, fallback ~/.claude/hooks/.events_cache.json. Fail-open, handlers called outside lock, 12 smoke tests pass.

Created tool_fingerprint.py SHA256 MCP tool fingerprinting for supply chain security. API: fingerprint_tool(name, desc, params)->hex, register_tool()->(is_new, changed, old_hash, new_hash), check_tool_integrity()->(matches, hashes), get_all_fingerprints()->dict, get_changed_tools()->list. Store: ~/.claude/.tool_fingerprints.json (atomic writes). Added 13 tests test_framework.py line 11727, all pass. Standalone run verified.

Gate 2 bypass audit (2026-02-20): Both bypasses already fixed. (1) FLAG-INTERLEAVING: _exec_is_safe() uses shlex.split(), scans ALL remaining args for -c/-e (lines 177-228). (2) HEREDOC: << regex r"(?<!<)<<(?!<)\\s*" line 70, _is_safe_exception() checks DANGEROUS_HEREDOC_CMDS (python, bash, etc). Test coverage: test_framework.py sections M1 (lines 1512-1535), M2 (1540-1546), 13 tests pass, syntax clean.

Enhanced audit_log.py gate decision logging. log_gate_decision() 3 new params: file_path, agent_id (fallback session_id), timestamp override. Entries written to hooks/.audit_trail.jsonl (append-only). get_recent_decisions(gate_name=None, limit=50): reads trail, returns newest-first, optional filter, returns [] if missing. Entry fields: id, timestamp, gate, tool, decision, reason, session_id, state_keys, severity, file_path, agent_id. Backward compat preserved, 9 tests pass.

Session 165 Sprint 2 Wave 3-4: 40+ agents deployed (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku). Created: tool_patterns.py, gate_dashboard.py, state_migrator.py, skill_health.py, pipeline_optimizer.py, gate_graph.py, event_replay.py, hook_profiler.py, session_analytics.py, gate_correlator.py, skill_mapper.py, hot_reload.py, memory_maintenance.py, mutation_tester.py, gate_18_canary.py. Pyright fixes, Gate 2 bypass tests, Gate 17 unicode/homoglyph. Gate 1 corruption from concurrent runners restored from git.

Created state_migrator.py (344 lines). Functions: migrate_state() adds missing fields/defaults, validate_state() returns (valid, errors, warnings), get_schema_diff() structured diff, validate_and_migrate() combines both, get_schema_metadata() API-friendly schema. Note: validate_state() needs flexible type checking—accept int/float for timestamps, None for optional (last_test_exit_code, recent_test_failure). Reads default_state, get_state_schema, STATE_VERSION, MAX_* from shared/state.py. Used by gates/boot.py.

Created fuzz_gates.py for adversarial robustness. First run (seed=12345, 500 iter/gate): 9/15 gates crashed, 20 total crashes in 3.995s. Root causes: (1) tool_input=None—gates call .get() without guard, (2) tool_input=list—non-dict crashes, (3) tool_name=None—Gate 17 startswith() without guard. Fix: if not isinstance(tool_input, dict): tool_input = {}. Features: --iterations, --seed, --gate, --quiet, JSON output, 6 strategies, adversarial generators.

Test run 2026-02-20: 1334 passed, 75 failed, 1409 total. Root causes: (1) State contamination—test sessions accumulate verified_fixes triggering Gate 6 blocks, (2) Gate 18 canary in dispatcher, (3) Error tracking failures, (4) Gate 1 intercepts when files not pre-read, (5) E1 crash test leaves __pycache__. Note: test_framework.py is standalone script, run: python3 hooks/test_framework.py (NOT pytest).

Gate 2 test additions: 103 new test cases (lines 330-551). (1) shlex bypass—16 cases (semicolons, pipes, backticks, command substitution, &&, ||, &), (2) obfuscated—21 cases (paths, flags, shred, find delete, truncate), (3) force push—19 blocked+4 safe (-f/-uf/-fu, --force-with-lease), (4) SQL comments—22 cases (C-style, double-dash, objects, case, whitespace), (5) git reset—18 blocked+4 safe. Fixed gate_02_no_destroy.py regex patterns. All 103 pass.

Test run 2026-02-20 post-Sprint-2: 51 unique failures. TRACKERS fail (tool_call_count, files_read, memory_queried, test_run, memory_last_queried)—state contamination. STATE CASCADE: Gate 1 BLOCKED instead of 5/6/7/8/9. BUGS: (1) Gate 6 plan mode—E1 __pycache__, (2) Read captured—pre-existing queue entries, (3) _extract_test_status—returns value without state, (4) get_most_used_tool—contamination, (5) Gate 7 tests fail (.cron.d, .env, .key, sudoers).

Session 168: Fixing 46 test failures. E1/E2 crash area (lines 1674-1723). Findings: (1) run_enforcer() subprocess (not import caching), (2) E1 replaces gate_01, restores, removes .pyc—cleanup OK, (3) E2 renames gate_01 to .hidden with guard, (4) Test format FAIL name or PASS name, (5) File 13,943 lines, (6) 3 categories: E1/E2 crash (1), Gate 1 cascade (40), Gate 18 mismatch (5). Need full test run output.
